message:
feat(fs): offload DirEntry metadata and file_type to blocking pool via Arc sharing

DirEntry::metadata() and file_type() previously called the blocking
std::fs methods synchronously on the async task thread, which can stall
the executor when the underlying filesystem is slow (NFS, FUSE, etc.).

Changes:
- Wrap the inner std::fs::DirEntry in Arc so it can be cheaply cloned
  and moved into a spawn_blocking_io closure without re-resolving the
  path or changing the std entry's semantics (important for symlinks
  where the dirfd+name pair matters)
- metadata() and file_type() now clone the Arc and call the std methods
  on the blocking pool, keeping the async task thread unblocked
- Remove the now-incorrect clippy::unused_async allows on both methods
- Add test_dir_entry_symlink_semantics (unix-only) verifying that
  symlink entries still report the correct file_type and that metadata
  follows the symlink target, matching std::fs::DirEntry behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
